message:
fix(comparison): Add defensive edge case handling to k-mer analysis functions

Mirror the WASM defensive programming in the TypeScript k-mer implementation
to handle degenerate inputs gracefully:

extractKmerSet():
- Return empty Set immediately if k < 1 or sequence.length < k
- Prevents empty loop iterations and potential edge cases

extractKmerFrequencies():
- Return empty Map immediately if k < 1 or sequence.length < k
- Consistent with extractKmerSet behavior

analyzeKmers():
- Return zero-initialized KmerAnalysis if k < 1
- All metrics (Jaccard, containment, cosine, Bray-Curtis) set to 0
- Prevents downstream calculations with invalid k values

These guards ensure:
- No runtime errors from negative k values
- No misleading results from sequences shorter than k
- Consistent behavior between TS and WASM implementations
- Graceful degradation instead of silent errors

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
